observations.recommendation: Conduct judicious planning and scheduling to ensure prompt implementation of its disaster prevention PPAs to optimally utilize the 70 per cent allocation of the LDRRMF.

Completely fill in the statutory positions of the LDRRMO in accordance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to ensure continuity of service along with the efficient and effective implementation of disaster risk management programs.

Submit its legal basis on the inclusion of the lot acquisition in the conduct of competitive bidding of the project and on the corresponding disbursement for the land acquisition as included in the progress payment.

Explain why the availment of the 15 percent mobilization fee by the contractor was allowed even in the absence of a valid supporting document. Since the advance payment is not yet completely recouped by the Municipality, submission of a valid bank guarantee/irrevocable letter of credit/surety bond is still required.

Obtain a formal estimate of recoverable service amount for the damaged items, compare with the carrying amount and make as basis in providing an allowance for impairment. Disclosures shall be provided in the financial statement in accordance with this standard. Management shall also consider immediate disposal of the above items.

Strictly comply with the documents required to support the payment of the purchase of land as prescribed under Presidential Decree (PD) 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the provisions of Chapter VIII (F) of Republic Act No. 8974 (Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations).

Submit the original copies of the disbursement voucher and supporting documents to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension/ Disallowance.

Fill in the vacant positions and provide appropriation for a more effective administration of those offices so that delivery of basic services needed by the constituents can be attained.

Likewise, the Budget Officer should allocate budget for those vacant positions to protect collapsing of Plantilla position.

Moreover, the Head of Personnel/ Human Resource Management Officer-Designate should publish the vacancy and if there will be no qualified applicant, recommend to the LCE for the temporary appointment of those willing and available applicant for the position.

Explain in writing, together with the required supporting documents, the non-compliance to the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, and to prospectively comply with the said law, to avoid suspension and/or disallowance.

Submit the LDRRMP to the office of the auditor for verification as well as the LDRRMFIP and the Monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month and henceforth, provisions of the Joint Circular of NDRRMC/DBM and DILG dated March 25, 2017 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, respectively, should be properly observed.
Process the titling of the lots and transfer the Tax Declaration of the two lots under the name of the Municipality to prevent third-party claims.
Submit the required documents and require the department heads to refund the unauthorized overtime paid totaling P111,691.00 pursuant to Sections 3.1.1 and 4.1.10 of Budget Circular No. 10.

Submit the required documents, otherwise, all those who authorized the payment of the subject allowance shall be held personally liable for the amount involved.

Likewise, the elective officials granted with CNA be directed to refund the amount received. A Notice of Disallowance will be issued in this regard.

Stop the practice of paying overtime pay to the Sanggunian Bayan Secretary and require the latter to refund the overtime paid of ₱30,528.00 being not authorized under Sections 3.1.1 and 4.1.10 of Budget Circular No. 10.

Check the completeness of the documents supporting a particular transaction before signing Certificate A of the voucher.

Submit the pertinent supporting documents to the transactions involving P18,228,152.04, otherwise, subject transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit.

See to it that all claims against the municipal funds are covered with duly supported complete documentation to evidence actual disbursement and delivery of items allegedly procured. A repetition of the same will be dealt with in accordance with prescribed laws, rules and regulations.

Prepare a written explanation on why the enumerated procurement of goods was made contrary to the quoted provisions of the Revised IRR (B) of RA 9184.

Observe faithful compliance with provisions of Revised IRR of RA 9184 and other pertinent laws and regulations governing procurement policies and procedures in their procurement of goods, infrastructure and consultancy.
Exert efforts to reconcile the general ledger and cashbook balances for the account, Cash in Vault to assure the accuracy of the reported balances.

We recommended that all concerned department heads and employees are required to refund the CNA Incentive they received at P24,000.00 each since the incentive was sourced from savings from allotment in CY 2008 instead of savings generated from cost-cutting measures and systems improvement, contrary to Budget Circular No. 2006-1 dated February 1, 2006. Moreover, department heads are not among those authorized to receive the incentive as set forth under item 3.0 of the same Circular.

The project employees should be required to refund the year-end bonus granted to them. The Municipal Mayor should be extra careful in the utilization of the 20% development fund. It should not include expenses for personal services in conformity with the guideline set under Joint Memorandum Circular No 1, s 2005 dated September 20, 2005.

Payment out of public funds should be made in pursuance of an appropriation law in accordance with Section 305 (a) of the Local Government Code of 1991. The municipal officials and employees concerned are required to refund the increase of RATA and the extra cash gift granted to them.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management, through the members of the Inventory Committee, agreed to:
Prioritize the compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to resolve the issues on non-conduct of physical count and unreconciled inventory balances;
We also reiterated our recommendation and Management, through the Municipal Treasurer, agreed to:
Maintain property cards for each class of PPE item; and
Adopt and assign the required property numbers and provide property tags/stickers for each PPE.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to initiate the request for write-off of the unclassified and dormant accounts following the documentary requirements and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2023-008, amending COA Circular No. 2016-005, if, despite concerted efforts, the source documents, records and details or composition of these accounts could no longer be traced and established.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to request from the former bookkeepers of the BWD the details/schedules of the accounts, especially the Other Receivables account, to identify the cause of the negative balance and effect the necessary adjustments to reflect the accurate balance of the affected account in order to fairly present the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials agreed to:
Secure authority from the Cebu Provincial Government to recognize income for the unexpended balances of the financial assistance and, if applicable, to realign unutilized grants to other priority projects of the Municipality; and
We also recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant, agreed to:
Reclassify the amount of ₱359,198.97 received from a private corporation to Trust Liabilities account, or Grants and Donations in Cash account, depending upon the nature and purpose of the transaction.

We recommended and Management, through the Municipal Accountant and implementing offices, agreed to process for the proper liquidation of the amount granted as well as the refund of excess project funds to the donor.

We recommended and Management, through the MDRRM Officer, agreed to submit the revised/supplemental LDRRMFIP and AIP for 2023 which includes the PPAs charged to the unexpended balances of prior years’ LDRRMF to substantiate the expenditures charged thereof pursuant to the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials agreed to initiate measures to completely fill in the statutory positions of the LDRRMO in accordance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to ensure continuity of service along with the efficient and effective implementation of disaster risk management programs.

Create an Inventory Committee, whose members shall be temporarily relieved from their regular duties, to facilitate the completion of the physical inventory of PPEs, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

Furnish the Municipal Accountant, at the beginning of the year, with a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year to be used as the basis in the setting up of RPT/SET Receivables.

Consider utilizing a collection information system to enhance the efficiency of the assessment and collection functions of the Municipality for a more improved fiscal administration.

Reconcile the records pertaining to the parcels of land owned by the Municipality and effect the necessary adjustments, if any, to present the correct Land account balance.

Make representation with the Registry of Deeds to request copies of all contracts of selling, transferring, or otherwise conveying, leasing, or mortgaging real property received by, or acknowledged before their office to serve as a reference/proof in the registration, transfer and titling of these parcels of land and ensure ownership and full protection of the interest of the Municipality in compliance with Section 39 of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 92-386.

Formulate a holistic/ comprehensive yet realistic plan to expedite the timely procurement and implementation of the identified priority development projects.

Conduct detailed engineering investigation and pre-planning prior to execution in order to achieve full implementation of the projects for a timely delivery of the desired services and benefits to its beneficiaries.

Employ regular monitoring mechanisms in assessing the rate of progress of project implementation in order to achieve the desired level of efficiency.

Submit justification on the non-imposition of liquidated damages from the contractor.  

Compute and recoup the overpayment, representing the unimposed liquidated damages from the contractor or from the person/s responsible for the non-imposition of the liquidated damages.

Submit copies of the SWA, which was prepared during the inspection of the individual projects at the expected date of completion, for the Audit Team’s verification of the computation of the liquidated damages.

Prepare a Job Order contract detailing the individual daily rate, period of service and statement of duties and responsibilities to be duly signed as agreed by both the JO worker and the LGU.  

Require the JO workers to prepare individual accomplishment reports which shall be reviewed and duly approved by their department heads so as to verify the actual works performed and to avoid any duplication of assignments/duties.

Submit a justification for the non-imposition of LD from the contractors.

Compute and recoup the overpayment, representing the unimposed LD from the contractors or from the person/s responsible for the non-imposition of the LD. Copies of SWA prepared during inspection of the individual projects at expected date of completion shall be submitted to the Audit Team for verification of the computed liquidated damages.

Prepare necessary adjusting entries to recognize impairment of the uncollectible accounts.  

Process for derecognition of dormant receivable accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 by filing a request for write-off to be supported by certified relevant documents validating the reason for non-collectability.

Submit a valid explanation on what controls had been made in ensuring that the relief goods were fully accounted for, delivered and actually received by the beneficiaries.

Submit reliable documents, if there are any, to prove the disposition and receipt of the quantities of relief supplies to and by the constituents.

Require the submission of the aforementioned supporting documents from all departments that facilitated the receipt and distribution of donations in-kind, prepare the required summary/list and submit to the Audit Team. The one-time separate reports for cash and in-kind donations shall also be prepared and submitted to the NDRRMC through the OCD.

Submit a written explanation on the inclusion of ineligible families in the distribution of the SAP cash assistance.

Send demand letters to the ineligible beneficiaries requiring the refund of the cash aid they received and ensure that the total amount of ₱2,676,000.00 is collected and returned to the DSWD.
Require the contractor a new performance security with validity up to the estimated time of issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance to protect the interest of the Municipality.

Accomplish the Contract of Agreement completely and require acknowledgement from the supplier.

Explain why the Municipal Accountant was not included in the contract of agreement as one of the witnesses.

The dates of any procurement document should not be taken too lightly. These will prove if the procuring entity adheres strictly to the implementing rules and regulations of R.A. 9184 as prescribed. Explain/justify the cause of inconsistencies of the dates in the above-mentioned documents.
Explain/justify why the supplier was not disqualified right away when it submitted an expired Tax Clearance.

Submit the lacking documents as stated and henceforth strictly follow the bidding and awarding process as provided in Sections 17.1, 25.1, 25.2 and 39.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184.

Strictly adhere to the aforementioned provisions of Section 89 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and provisions of Circular No. 97-002 to ensure proper granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the compliance of COA Circular 2020-006 for the cleansing of PPE accounts. We enjoin the Municipal Mayor to create a new Inventory Committee whose members shall be temporarily relieved of their regular duties to complete the physical inventory in three months or less. The Inventory Committee shall prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities, and shall ensure its full implementation.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to recognize impairment of the uncollectible accounts. Thereafter, Management shall process derecognition of dormant receivable accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 by filing a request for write-off to be supported by certified relevant documents validating the reason for non-collectability.

We recommended that Management submit its legal basis on the inclusion of the lot acquisition in the conduct of competitive bidding of the project and on the corresponding disbursement for the land acquisition as included in the progress payment.

Management claimed that the contractors had requested suspensions of work/ request for extensions for the mentioned projects.
Auditor’s rejoinder:
The Audit Team had previously requested for the submission of approved work suspensions /requests for extensions granted by Management for the subject contracts but none were submitted during the audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure compliance with Section 62 of the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184 and require the suppliers to post warranty security in its required form and amount before payment, to safeguard the interest of the government.

We recommended and Management agreed to investigate the responsible persons why the project was declared as 100 percent completed to determine accountability. We recommended and Management agreed to establish implementing guidelines on the grant of assistance to individuals/family in crisis situation which will clearly set the qualification of the indigent, amount and frequency of assistance, documentary requirements and the like, concurred by the Sangguniang Bayan, to provide proper control in the disbursement of public funds.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the MDRRMO to submit a supplemental LDRRMF investment plan for CY 2021 with MDRRMC approval to support the disbursements for projects charged to the unexpended balance. Henceforth, the MDRRMC shall ensure that the annual investment plan shall cover all projects and activities to be funded from the current year’s budget for LDRRMF and all unexpended balance from prior years.

We recommended and Management agreed to coordinate with NTC to work out on other legal procedures to authorize the purchase and use of the radio communication devices in the absence of the purchaser’s and dealer’s permits.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the end-users and the BAC to stop the practice of referring to brand names in the preparation of the RFQ. Instead, use relevant characteristics, functionality and/or performance requirements for the goods to be procured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Team to complete the physical count of all PPE, prepare the RPCPPE identifying items still used in the operation, unserviceable or already impaired and conduct reconciliation with the accounting records. In addition, direct the Accounting Department to prepare and maintain updated Property Ledger Cards of all the properties of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC submit a valid justification why the procurement regulation on the conduct of a pre-procurement conference for projects costing more than P5 million was not complied.

We recommended that Management explain why the availment of the 15 percent mobilization fee by the contractor was allowed even in the absence of a valid supporting document. Since the advance payment is not yet completely recouped by the Municipality, submission of a valid bank guarantee/irrevocable letter of credit/surety bond is still required.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractor a new performance security with validity up to the estimated time of issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance only after the defects liability period or one year from date of completion has lapsed, to guarantee that defects in the construction works within this period are rectified at the contractor’s expense.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to obtain a formal estimate of recoverable service amount for the damaged items, compare with the carrying amount and make as basis in providing an allowance for impairment. Disclosures shall be provided in the financial statement in accordance with this standard. Management shall also consider immediate disposal of the above items.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant or authorized representative to submit valid justification on the delayed submission of disbursement vouchers/supporting documents for cash advances intended for confidential activities to the COA audit team assigned. Henceforth, submission of said documents must be within prescribed deadline to avoid administrative disciplinary actions and disallowance of cash advance pursuant to Item 8 of the joint circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure immediate submission of the complete set of the 2018 Annual and Supplemental Budgets reviewed already by the Provincial Budget Office, and the BAC Chairman to submit immediately the 2018 APP and its supporting PPMPs for audit verification, to avoid disallowance of transactions. We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant submit a new SCBAA with budget amounts based on correct figures from the Annual and Supplemental Budgets.

We also recommended that henceforth, management observe faithful compliance with provisions of Revised IRR of RA 9184 and other pertinent laws and regulations governing procurement policies and procedures in their procurement of goods, infrastructure and consultancy.

observations.recommendation:

We also recommended that management see to it that all disbursements for procurement are supported with complete, properly filled-up, duly signed and dated documentation as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure immediate submission of the complete set of the 2018 Annual and Supplemental Budgets reviewed already by the Provincial Budget Office, and the BAC Chairman to submit immediately the 2018 APP and its supporting PPMPs for audit verification, to avoid disallowance of transactions. We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant submit a new SCBAA with budget amounts based on correct figures from the Annual and Supplemental Budgets.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly disclose in the notes to financial statements at year end reporting date, the contingent liability arising from the billed interests, penalty and other fees for this loan obligation as these are of material amounts for which the possibility of an outflow of resources is not remote. The disclosure should include a brief description of the nature of the contingent liability and where practicable, an estimate of its financial effects measured.

We recommended that Management conduct competitive bidding or any alternative mode when justified by conditions in the procurement of catering services for its various activities following the guidelines prescribed in GPPB Resolution No. 12-2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant correct the erroneous recording of the settlement of the assumed loan obligation to LWUA which was debited to Due from Joint Venture, instead of Loans Payable-Domestic account. We also recommended that the recording of interest payable on borrowings be in accordance with the accrual basis of accounting and see to it that only interest expense incurred but not yet paid as of balance sheet date are included.

We recommended that the BAC submit a valid explanation in regards to the observed deficiency. Prospectively, BAC should ensure that contracts are awarded to bidders who comply with the submission of bid security and all other requirements of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to avoid disallowance.

We recommended that the BAC follow the Revised IRR and require the holding of pre-procurement conference for procurement of infrastructure projects costing above ₱5M, and procurement of goods costing above ₱2M as this will determine the agency’s readiness in the procurement project at hand in terms of its legal, technical and financial requirements. Even if the project cost is below the threshold, the BAC is still encouraged to conduct a pre-procurement conference if the circumstances, like the complexity of the technical specifications, warrant the holding of such a conference before the agency proceeds with the procurement.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) ensure that all the provisions of the Philippine Bidding Documents are complied, such that all the required bidding documents are issued to the bidders and completely submitted during conduct of procurement of infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management expedite the implementation of the said projects following its existing guidelines so that the immediate benefits can be delivered to the constituents in the soonest possible time. We also recommended that Management submit the Memorandum of Agreement entered into between the LGU and the NGAs, and other pertinent documents relating to the fund transfers and implementation for audit verification.

We recommended that the Municipality submit a written explanation why no warranty security was required from the contractors for the foregoing infrastructure projects. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that full payment of infrastructure projects be supported with warranty security in compliance with the regulation.

We recommended that the municipal officials concerned adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG – DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for development projects.  Likewise, proper planning be made for the full utilization of the fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the BAC to follow the bidding procedures in the procurement of
the learning materials in accordance with the rules and regulations of Republic
Act 9184. Explain/justify the inconsistencies of the dates in the preparation of the
procurement particularly in the delivery of the items procured. Likewise, submit
the list of beneficiaries/recipients of the above-mentioned materials and strictly
follow the provision of Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 to avoid issuing Notice of
Suspension/Notice of Disallowance.

We recommend that the Municipality establish Local Council for the Protection of
Children (LCPC) and allocate 1% of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the
purpose in compliance with the above provisions of RA 9344 and DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120. Further, we request the submission of the
2017 Annual Budget in order not to invalidate the transaction of the agency.

We recommend that the requisitioning officer submit a written explanation on
non-compliance with the applicable provisions of EO 49. Also, explain/justify why
the License to Operate of the establishment was license to operate as Medical
Device Distributor instead of drugs and medicines.

We recommend that the GAD Focal Person be directed to submit the certification
of the DILG approving the GAD Plan and Budget to ensure that the activities
therein are in consonance with the criteria stipulated in RA 9710 addressing
gender issues. Non-submission of the said certification maybe a ground for the
issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommend that the statutory positions required under the NDRRMC-
DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) dated April 4, 2014 be
filled up immediately by the Municipality, instead of designating existing
employees to comply with NDRRMC–DILG)–DBM–CSC Joint Memorandum
Circular (JMC) No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommend that the Municipal Local Chief Executive as well as the
Legislative officer should fill up the vacant positions and should provide
appropriation for a more effective administration of those offices so that delivery
of basic services needed by the constituents can be attained. Likewise, the
Budget Officer should allocate budget for those vacant positions to protect
collapsing of Plantilla position.

Moreover, the Head of Personnel/Human Resource Management Officer-
Designate should publish the vacancy and if there will be no qualified applicant,
recommend to the Local Chief Executive for the temporary appointment of
those willing and available applicant for the position.

We recommend that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee to
explain in writing, together with the required supporting documents, the non-
compliance to the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184, and to prospectively comply
with the said law, to avoid suspension and/or disallowance.

We recommend management to strictly comply with the documents required to
support the payment of the purchase of land as prescribed under Presidential Decree
(PD) 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the provisions of Chapter VIII (F) of
Republic Act No. 8974 (Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment
Operations).

Further, we require management to submit the original copies of the
disbursement voucher and supporting documents to avoid issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to explain in
writing the reasons in the non-compliance of the provisions of the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA 9184. Furthermore, BAC should diligently observe the provisions of
the said law, and see to it that the required documents are in order and are
timely complied, to avoid speculations of irregularities from the public.

We recommend management to submit its composition of the Municipal Local
School Board as embodied in Section 98 of Republic Act 7160 to avoid issuing
Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.

We recommend that Management submit the Memorandum of Agreement
(MOA) duly executed by and between the Municipality of Borbon, Cebu and the
source agency and the corresponding Special Allotment Release Order (SARO)
to determine the standards of implementation of the different projects and
whether the projects were implemented in accordance with such standards.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive to submit the Annual Budget within the
prescribed date and advised the Municipal Budget Officer to be vigilant on the
dates of the preparation the budget. Further, the above budgetary documents and
forms should be properly filled-up/accomplished by the Budget Officer.

We recommend management to submit the required documents to support
payment of the loan amortizations as well as the agreement between the
Municipality and the LWUA as prescribed under Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and
Section 344 of RA 7160.

We recommend that the Agency comply all the documentary requirements in
the procurement of firearms and bullet-proof vests as prescribed in the
Philippine National Police Memorandum Circular No. 2012-011 dated October

Implementation of the Standard Operating Procedure No. 13 and the
Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No.10591, otherwise
known as “Comprehensive Firearms and Ammunition Regulation Act.”
Likewise, strict compliance of the procurement process in accordance with the
Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 should be
observed.

be taken too lightly. These will prove if the procuring entity adhere strictly to the
implementing rules and regulations of R.A. 9184 as prescribed. Explain/justify

observations.recommendation:
We again reiterate our recommendation that the Local School Board limit the
utilization of the Special Education Fund to the programs and projects defined
under Section 1 of Republic Act No. 5447 and the priority listing provided in
Section 100 and Section 272 of Republic Act (RA) 7160 otherwise known as the
Local Government Code, and Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 of DECS, DBM
and DILG, in order to maximize the benefits that the public school students could
avail under this fund.

We recommend that Management should comply with the provisions of Section 9
of Republic Act No. 5447 on the timely remittance of the Special Education Fund.

We recommend that the LDRRMO submit the LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report
of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal
Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Henceforth, the
provisions of Par. 5.1.2 and 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September
12, 2012 should be properly observed as well as the Implementing Rules and
Regulations of Republic Act 10121, otherwise known as the Philippine Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010.

We recommend that the municipal officials concerned adhere strictly to the
provisions of DILG – DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the
guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of 20% of the Annual Internal
Revenue Allotment (IRA) for development projects. Likewise, proper planning be
made for the full utilization of the fund.

We recommend that management strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential
Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular Nos. 85-55-A. Also, exercise due prudence so
that government funds are safeguarded and conserved to avoid unnecessary,
extravagant and excessive expenditures. Also, send only those officials and
personnel to trainings and seminars relevant to the nature of their work.

We recommend that management see to it that all disbursements for procurement
are supported with complete, properly filled-up documentation as required under
Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We also recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee/BAC Secretariat see to
it that all documents in any stage of the procurement process are complete,
properly filled-up and dated pursuant to the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee/BAC Secretariat ensure
that the Procuring Entity abides by the standards set forth in the Procurement
Law to avoid any error in any stage of the procurement process which may
prejudice the interest of the Municipality.

We also recommend that management see to it that all disbursements for
procurement are supported with complete, properly filled-up, duly signed and
dated documentation as required under Section 4(6) of PD 1445. Further,
management shall see to it that all documents in any stage of the procurement
process are properly filled-up and dated pursuant to the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
refrain from processing the payment for salaries and wages without the
accomplishment reports prepared by the job order concerned to facilitate the
review of the claims pursuant to Section 2 of PD 1445.

We recommend for an immediate submission of the CY 2016 Annual Budget to
facilitate the review of the municipality’s transactions whether expenditures are in
accordance with the budget.

We recommend that the Municipality strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections
7.1, 7.2 and 7.3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act 9184
mandating that all government procurement shall be in accordance with the
Annual Procurement Plan of the procuring entity. Likewise, the Municipal
Mayor, as the Head of the Procuring Entity, should ensure proper compliance
therewith.

We recommend that the Agency Head
require the Accountant, Budget Officer
and Treasurer to strictly comply with the
regulations on the submission of financial
reports particularly COA Circular 2010-
001, Section 6.05 of COA Circular 95-006
and Section 70 of the NGAS Manual for
LGU, Volume I. Otherwise, institute
appropriate administrative sanctions
pursuant to Section 122 (2) of PD 1445.
